full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Getting a structural reference before writing my own implementation.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: can you give me pseudocode for the 1D coordinate ascent loop from this SVM paper, just the loop structure not actual python code
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I wrote the full Python class from scratch. Used the pseudocode only as a reference to check I had the right order of operations. Added the incremental f_vals update, clipping, and K_ii safety guard myself.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed the loop order: compute gradient → check KKT violation → if converged stop → pick i_star → update alpha → update f_vals.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified each step against Algorithm 1 in the paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: my max_violation keeps jumping around and not decreasing, i think my f_vals update is wrong. im doing f_vals += (alpha_new - alpha_old) * K[i_star, :]. is this right?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Found the bug myself first (missing y[i_star]). LLM confirmed my fix was correct.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed the correct line is f_vals += (alpha_new - alpha_old) * y[i_star] * K[i_star, :]. The label y[i_star] must be included since K stores raw kernel values not label-weighted ones.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Tested and confirmed max_violation decreases monotonically after fix.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Naive question about what alpha values mean.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: quick dumb q: if alpha_i is 0 does that point have no effect on the classifier at all?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — just a conceptual check to make sure I understood correctly.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed yes — points with alpha=0 are not support vectors and contribute zero to the decision function. Only points with alpha > 0 matter.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Matched what I knew about SVM support vectors.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking the citation for the update rule.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: should i cite Eq 3 or Procedure 1 for the alpha update step in my notebook annotations
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified: Eq (3) is the dual objective. The actual update step is in Procedure 1 Section 2. Updated my annotation to cite Procedure 1.

top_5_prompts.prompt: my f_vals update seems wrong, im doing f_vals += (alpha_new - alpha_old) * K[i_star, :], is this right?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This caught the bug that was breaking my solver. Without fixing this, nothing in Tasks 2.3, 3.1, and 3.2 would have worked correctly.

top_5_prompts.prompt: can you give me pseudocode for the 1D coordinate ascent loop, just the structure not python code
top_5_prompts.why_important: Gave me a reference to check my own implementation structure against, which I then built myself.

top_5_prompts.prompt: should i cite Eq 3 or Procedure 1 for the alpha update step
top_5_prompts.why_important: Task 2.2 is graded on annotation quality — getting the paper reference right matters.
